paper_metadata.title: DQ-BART: Efficient Sequence-to-Sequence Model via Joint Distillation and Quantization
paper_metadata.abstract: Large-scale pre-trained sequence-to-sequence models like BART and T5 achieve state-of-the-art performance on many generative NLP tasks. However, such models pose a great challenge in resource-constrained scenarios owing to their large memory requirements and high latency. To alleviate this issue, we propose to jointly distill and quantize the model, where knowledge is transferred from the full-precision teacher model to the quantized and distilled low-precision student model. Empirical analyses show that, despite the challenging nature of generative tasks, we were able to achieve a 16.5x model footprint compression ratio with little performance drop relative to the full-precision counterparts on multiple summarization and QA datasets. We further pushed the limit of compression ratio to 27.7x and presented the performance-efficiency trade-off for generative tasks using pre-trained models. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first work aiming to effectively distill and quantize sequence-to-sequence pre-trained models for language generation tasks.

extraction_data.innovation: Joint distillation and quantization via distillation-aware quantization, which enables efficient compression of BART models while preserving performance through a teacher-student framework with MSE-based distillation losses on logits, attention, and hidden states.
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Next-token prediction (via masked language modeling and sequence-to-sequence generation)
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Large-scale text corpus (implied from context; not explicitly named)
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised fine-tuning on summarization and QA tasks
extraction_data.extension: Distillation-aware quantization integrates model distillation and weight quantization in a unified training loop, where the full-precision student model is quantized at each iteration to guide low-precision updates.
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficient deployment of large pre-trained sequence-to-sequence models in resource-constrained scenarios
extraction_data.application: Text summarization, long-form question answering, machine translation
extraction_data.paper_title: DQ-BART: Efficient Sequence-to-Sequence Model via Joint Distillation and Quantization
